def: part. Frightened, scared, alarmed, afraid.
part. Frightened, alarmed; fearful, trembling; — s.m. Timid person, trembler, &c.
adj. & s.m. Protecting, defending; — protector, defender, guardian, deliverer, saviour.
adj. & s.m. Protecting, defending; — protector, defender, guardian, deliverer, saviour.
adj. Triple, threefold, consisting of three; divided into three parts; (in comp.) three ( = tri); — s.m. Three collectively, a triad, triplet
subs: traikatva, s.m. The union of three in one, trinity in unity
s.m. The rule of three (in Arith.)
s.f. Three lines, &c. ( = tri-balī); three rows
s.m. The ruler of trilok or the three worlds, an epithet of Indra
s.m. The three sciences; the three Vedas; study or knowledge of the three Vedas; one versed in the three Vedas; one who is acquainted with three sciences.

def: adj. & s.m. Preserving, defending, delivering, rescuing, freeing; — preserver, &c. ( = trāṇī).
s.f. A triad, triplet; the three Vedas collectively (i.e. the Rig, Yajur, and Sāma); — the plant Conyzā serratula
subs: s.m. The duties, forms of sacrifice, &c. prescribed by the three Vedas.

def: adj. Thirteenth.
s.f. The thirteenth day of the lunar fortnight.
adj. Twenty-third.
adj. Twenty-three.
adj. = tirsaṭh, q.v.

s.f. A collection or assemblage of three, a triad; the three sacred fires collectively (i.e. the southern, household, and sacrificial fires); the second yug or silver age of the Hindūs (comprising 1,296,000 years)
subs: (˚tā+ag˚), s.m. The three sacred fires collectively ( = agni-tretā); one who has preserved the three sacred fires.

def: adj. Three (used in comp.)
s.f. lit. 'Triple-braid'; the place (now called Allahabad) where the Ganges unites with the Jamnā and is supposed to receive underground the Sarasvatī; the confluence of three sacred rivers.
adj. Having three curves or bends (as have many images of Kr̤ishna, i.e. with the legs, loins, and neck bent); standing awry; — s.m. A person standing awry.
adj. Having three curves or bends (as have many images of Kr̤ishna, i.e. with the legs, loins, and neck bent); standing awry; — s.m. A person standing awry.
adj. & s.f. Having the position described under tri-bhaṅga, an epithet of Kr̤ishṇa
subs: consisting of four lines, each of thirty-two syllabic instants.

def: s.m. The three worlds (heaven, earth, and the lower regions), the universe (syn. tri-lok)
subs: s.m. The splendour of the three worlds
tri-bhuvan-nāth, s.m. Lord of the three worlds, epithet of Vishṇu
adj. Possessed of the three worlds, an epithet of Indra
s.f. The beauty of the three worlds; a woman of rare beauty.

def: adj. & s.m. Having three arms; three-sided, trilateral; — a three-sided figure, triangle.
s.f. The creeping plant Cissus pedata.

s.m. A wandering mendicant or devotee who has resigned worldly pursuits and carries three long bamboo staves tied together in his right hand; — the religious man who has his words, thoughts, and actions (or his mind, body, and speech) under control.
adj. & s.m. Thirty; — a name for the thirty-three deities (not including Brahmā, Vishṇu, and Śiva); — a god, a deity, an immortal; the abode of the thirty-three gods, heaven
subs: s.m. The food of the gods, ambrosia or amr̤it.

def: s.m. The Hindu Triad Brahmā, Vishṇu and Śiva.
adv. In three ways; in three parts; in three places; threefold, triply, trebly.
s.m. Disorder of the three humors of the body, vitiation of the bile, blood, and phlegm.
s.m. The aggregate of the three objects of existence, viz. virtue, pleasure, and wealth (syn. tri-varg, q.v.).
s.m. The country of the Trigartas, the modern Lahore.
s.f. A lascivious woman, a wanton.

s.m. The three qualities or constituents of nature and every existing thing; — any creature possessing those three qualities; — adj. Consisting of three threads or strings; threefold, triple, treble, thrice; possessing the three guṇas or qualities
subs: s.m. One who possesses the three guṇas or properties, an epithet of man.

def: s.m. The triple world ( = tri-bhuvan, and tri-lok, qq.v.).
s.m. The triple world ( = tri-bhuvan, and tri-lok, qq.v.).
s.f. The sine of ninety degrees, the radius (of a circle).
s.f. A triangular frame or bar across the mouth of a well (over which passes the rope of the bucket); a frame at the bottom of a well (on which the masonry rests).
adj. & s.m. Triple, threefold; forming a triad; — the aggregate of three, a triad; the chin-bone; the hip.
s.m. The three times or tenses, the past, present, and future; morning, noon, and evening
subs: (vulg. tri-kāla-gya), adj. & s.m. Knowing the three times, omniscient; — a divine sage; a name of Buddh; a deity
adj. & s.m. Seeing the past, present, and future; omniscient; — a Rishi or divine sage; a name of Buddha; the Omniscient.

def: s.m. A kind of fish (Silurus); a species of plant ( = tri-kaṭ; and gokhrū).
s.m. The plant Ruellia longifolia.
s.m. The aggregate of three spices, viz. black pepper, long pepper, and dry ginger.
adj. & s.m. Three-cornered, triangular; — a triangle; the vulva
subs: or tri-koṇ-mitī vidyā, s.f. The science of trigonometry (syn. ilm-ě-mus̤allas).

def: adj. & s.m. Having three peaks; — a three-peaked mountain; name of several mountains in India; — sea-salt prepared by evaporation.
s.m. The aggregate of three spices, viz. black pepper, long pepper, and dry ginger.
adj. & s.m. Tri-ocular, three-eyed; — the three-eyed one, an epithet of Śiva.
s.m. The three worlds, i.e. the sky, atmosphere, and earth; or heaven, earth, and the lower region; — the universe; — an inhabitant of the three worlds
subs: s.m. Lord of the three worlds, an epithet of Indra.

def: s.f. The universe ( = tri-lok), the aggregate of the three worlds
subs: s.m. Lord of the universe, an epithet of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.

def: s.m. lit. 'The three sweet substances, sugar, honey, and ghī'; a portion of the Rig-veda beginning with the word madhu; one who knows or recites the three verses beginning with madhu.
s.f. Three ways or paths; the meeting of three roads ( = tri-path; ti-rāhā).
adj. & s.m. Pertaining to, or consisting of a protracted vowel (i.e. a vowel lengthened to three mātrās or prosodial instants); a vowel so lengthened ( = plut).
adj. &c. = tri-mukh, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Three-faced; having three mouths; — the confluence of three rivers.
s.m. A mountain with three peaks ( = tri-kūṭ).
adj. Having or assuming three forms or shapes (as Brahmā, Vishṇu, and Śiva); — s.m. a Buddha; a Jain saint; — s.f. Trinity, the Hindū triad.
s.m. Grass; a herb or any gramineous plant; a blade of grass; straw
subs: (˚na+ag˚), s.m. A grass fire, i.e. one quickly extinguished; conflagration of straw or chaff; burning a criminal wrapped up in straw
s.m. 'Pressing as close as grass,' close combat
adj. & s.m. Feeding on grass, graminivorus; — a graminivorous animal
s.m. 'King of plants'; the palm or palmyra tree, Borassus flabelliformis
adj. Abounding in grass, grassy; made of grass
tr̤iṇ-vat, tr̤iṇ-vant, adj. Abounding in grass, grassy; — tr̤iṇ-vat, adj. Grass-like; like a straw; insignificant, worthless.

def: s.m. See s.v. tr̤in.
adj. Grassy; made with grass.
s.f. (tr̤iṇatva, m.), The state or property of grass or herbage, gramineousness.
s.m. = tri-nāćiket, q.v.
adv. See s.v. tr̤iṇ.
adj. & s.m. = tri-loćan, q.v.
adj. Thirtieth.
adj. Thirty.
s.m. corr. of tri-bhuvan, q.v.
s.m. lit. 'Three-footed'; epithet of Fever personified as a demon or evil spirit, and represented with three feet and three hands (probably symbolizing the cold, hot, and sweating stages of fever).
s.m. A tripod; — adj. (in Math.) Trinomial.
s.f. The girth of an elephant; — the creeper Cissus pedata.
s.m. The fore-head marked naturally with three lines or wrinkles.
s.m. A place where three roads meet ( = tirāhā).
s.m. A building with three doors or gates.
s.m. A medicine composed of the three myrobalans, Terminalia chebula, T. bellerica, and Phyllanthus emblica.
part. Satiated, satisfied, contented
subs: v.t. To satisfy, to render content.

def: s.f. Satisfaction, contentment, satiety, repletion; pleasure, gratification.
s.m. Three curved hortzontal marks made across the forehead with the ashes of burnt cow-dung, sandal, &c., by the followers of Śiva or Śakti (and indispensable in proceeding to worship Śiva).
s.m. Three curved hortzontal marks made across the forehead with the ashes of burnt cow-dung, sandal, &c., by the followers of Śiva or Śakti (and indispensable in proceeding to worship Śiva).
s.m. = H ترراتري त्रिरात्रि tri-rātri, त्रिरात्रीtri-rātrī, s.f. Three nights collectively; consecutively; the space or duration of three nights.
s.f. = S ترراتره त्रिरात्र tri-rātra, s.m. Three nights collectively; consecutively; the space or duration of three nights.
s.f. = S ترسندهيه त्रिसन्ध्य tri-sandhya, s.m. The three periods or divisions of the day, viz., dawn, noon, and evening or sunset.
s.m. = S ترسندهيا त्रिसन्ध्या tri-sandhyā, s.f. The three periods or divisions of the day, viz., dawn, noon, and evening or sunset.
s.m. (With Buddhists) The three places of refuge, viz. Buddha, the Law, and the Assembly: — a Buddha; a sanctified teacher of the Jain sect.
s.f. Thirst; strong desire, longing; cupidity, avarice; — the plant Commelina salicifolia
subs: (˚shā+ār˚), adj. Suffering from thirst, thirsty; affected by desire, tortured by strong desire
tr̤ishā-want, adj. Thirsty; desirous, longing for.

def: part. adj. Thirsting, athirst, thirsty; desirous (of), longing (for), cupidinous.
s.f. Thirst; strong desire; ambition; cupidity, avarice
subs: (˚nā+ār˚), adj. Very thirsty, oppressed with thirst; tortured with desire
s.m. Cessation of desire, tranquillity of mind, contentment, resignation, patience.

def: adj. Thirsty (physically or metaphorically); desirous, longing for, cupidinous.
s.f. A sort of metre in which the stanza consists of three lines of various lengths.
adj. & s.m. Three-headed, having three tops or points; — name of an Asur killed by Vishṇu; name of a Rāksha killed by Rāma.
adj. & s.m. Having three horns or peaks; — a hill with three peaks; a triangle.
adj. & s.m. Having three horns; — a species of fish, Cyprinus rohita, or C. denticulatus.
s.m. A trident, a three-pointed pike or spear (especially the weapon of Śiva)
subs: adj. Trident in hand, an epithet of Śiva.

def: s.m. = tri-śūl, q.v.
s.m. The trident-bearer, an epithet of Śiva.
s.f. = tritwa, q.v.
s.m. An assemblage or collection of three, a triad, trinity.
adj. & s.m. Third; — a third
subs: s.f. A eunuch; a hermaphrodite; (in Gram.) the neuter gender.

def: adj. & s.m. Holding the third place or rank; having or receiving a third as one's share; — a person of the third rank; one who is entitled to a third part.
s.m. An assemblage or collection of three, a triad, trinity.
s.f. The three folds or wrinkles across the abdomen ( = tri-balī).

s.m. An aggregate of three things or substances, &c.; the three objects or pursuits of life, viz. religion or virtue (dharm), pleasure (kām), and wealth (arth); the three conditions of a king or of a state, viz. progress (vr̤iddhi), remaining stationary (sthāna), and decline (kshay), or gain, equality, and loss; the three qualities of nature, viz. purity, blindness, and depravity; the three higher castes or tribes.
s.m. The plant Ruella longifolia; the three myrobalans ( = tri-phalā); the three spices.
s.m. The three Vedas, viz. Rig, Yajus, and Sāman.
adj. & s.m. Familiar or acquainted with the three Vedas; — one who is acquainted with the three Vedas.
s.f. See the com. form tri-benī.
adj. Of three kinds; threefold, triple; in three ways
subs: s.f. The three kinds of suffering (i.e. dehī tāp, or that which is occasioned by the body; daivit-tāp, or that which comes from Providence, as calamity, &c.; and bhavatik-tāp, or that which arises from existence, or from contact with the world).

def: s.m. The three steps or strides (of Vishṇu); one who makes three steps or strides, an epithet of Vishṇu (who paced the three worlds in three steps in his Vāman avatār or 'dwarf incarnation').
s.f. The pot-herb Hincha repens.
s.f. A woman, female; a wife
subs: s.f. The science or knowledge of women
vulg. tiryā-ćillattar, s.m. Women's ways; woman's wiles or artfulness
triyā-rājya, s.m. Woman's rule; the kingdom of women, Amazon country; wife-rule, petticoat-government
s.f. Woman's persistency, female persistence, or importunity, &c.

def: s.f. lit. 'Containing three watches'; night; — an epithet of the river Jamna.
s.m. Sin (ʻthe impeder of the three objects of life').
s.m. A species of medicinal plant, Mollugo pentaphylla.
s.m. A minor drama; (in Pros.) a species of metre.
s.f. A rāginī or one of the female personifications of music.
s.f. Beak or bill (of a bird); mouth (of a fish); a kind of pike, Esox kankila.
s.f. A very minute space of time, a moment; loss, destruction (cf. ṭoṭā).

ety: Ap. Prk. तुहुं or तुहं, fr. Prk. base तु(S.त्व)+gen. aff. हुं, &c.
def: pron. Thou (used to imply depreciation or contempt; or by way of familiarity, or endearment; or, in addressing the Deity, to imply extreme reverence)
subs: tū-tū karnā, tū-karnā, v.t. To thou and thee, to address rudely or disrespectfully; to wrangle, quarrel; to abuse
s.f. 'Thou thou, I I'; altercation, wrangling, squabbling; abusing
v.t. To wrangle, squabble; to abuse
ex: اے خدا تو ہی میرا مددگار ہے!
ai xudā tū-hī merā madadgār hai!
O God, only Thou art my helper!
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुहुं (tuhũ), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑀁 (tumaṃ), 𑀢𑀼𑀁 (tuṃ), from Sanskrit त्वम् (tvam).

def: s.f. A kind of pulse, Cajanus indicus, or Cytisus cajan (syn. arhar).

ety: v.n. fr. طيب 'to be good or pleasant,' &c.
def: s.m. Name of a tree in Paradise, the fruit of which is said to be most delicious; — adj. Excellent, sweet, delicious, &c.
s.f. Sound of guggling or gurgling, gurgle.
s.f. = tumṛī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Light, worthless, insignificant; low, mean, base; — a low or mean fellow; a rake, a blackguard (cf. luććā).
adj. Void, empty; vain, worthless, insignificant, of no account, good-for-nothing; contemptible, despicable, low, vile
subs: v.t. To hold light, to consider of no account, to despise
s.m. lit. 'The sapless tree,' the castor-oil tree, Ricinus communis

def: adj. Worthless, insignificant, contemptible, base.
s.f. Emptiness; worthlessness, despicableness.
s.m. Heap, mound; stack; a mud pillar, boundary pillar, land-mark (syn. ḍholā); a mark or butt (to shoot at)
subs: s.f. Laying down boundary marks, marking a boundary
s.m.f. 'A mass of calumny,' a great calumniator.

def: s.m. pl. of tūda, q.v.

ety: formed in imitation of اف uff, q.v.; cf. also A. tufl
def: s.m. Spit, spittle; spray, foam (as of the sea); a curse; — intj. Shame!; fie!; a curse (on)!; ugh!; foh!; faugh! (e.g. tuf hai uskī auqāt par; aise nikhaṭṭū marduʼe-kī rīsh-par tuf hai).

r: t̤ufail, t̤ifl, Tufail, wasīla, z̤arīʻa, t̤ufail-se, kisī-ke
def: (dim. of t̤ifl, q.v.), s.m. & f. A companion, a humble follower; a parasite; a glutton; one who uninvited accompanies one of the guests to a feast, one who sponges (in this sense said to be derived from Tufail, a poet of Kūfā, who was noted as a feast-hunter); cause, means, instrumentality, intervention (syn. wasīla; z̤arīʻa)
subs: (kisī-ke), By means (of), through the merits or agency (of), &c.

def: adj. Sponging, parasitical.
s.m. One who sponges, &c. ( = t̤ufail).

ety: v.n. fr. طوف 'to surround, go round, envelop,' &c.
def: s.m. A violent storm of wind and rain, a tempest, typhoon; a flood, deluge, inundation; the universal deluge; a flood or torrent (of obloquy, &c.); — a commotion, noise, riot; a gross wrong; calumny (syn. andher); a great trial, or affliction, or calamity; a mischievous imp; a firebrand
subs: v.n. To raise a commotion, make a great noise, &c.
or t̤ūfān lagānā, or t̤ūfān lenā (-par), To asperse, calumniate, &c. ( = t̤ūfān bāṅdhnā)
v.n. = t̤ūfān uṭhānā and t̤ūfān karnā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طوفان (tūfān), from Arabic طُوفَان (ṭūfān).

def: s.m. A tube through which a thing is blown or propelled; a musket; pea-shooter; pop-gun.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تفنگ (tufang), perhaps from Khorezmian Turkic, but more likely via Chagatai through the Mughal Empire whose rulers were initially Chagatai-speaking, and established one of the three "gunpowder empires" alongside the Ottoman Empire and Safavid Iran. The word is itself derived as an onomatopoeia mimicking the sound of blowpipes used to kill birds.

def: s.m. A musketeer, matchlock-man.

adj. & s.m. Stormy, tempestuous; of or relating to a deluge; (met.) boisterous, quarrelsome, violent, noisy, riotous; slanderous; — a boisterous fellow; a mischief-making person, an incendiary; a slanderer, calumniator.

ety: for A. طفولية t̤ufūlīyat, fr. طفل
def: s.f. State or condition of an infant, or of a child; infancy; childhood.
s.f. The manna of bamboos ( = tabāshīr)

ety: said to be corr. fr. A. طغرة t̤ag̠rat, fr. طغر, a dial. var., or a corr. of دغر 'to thrust'; 'to press'; &c.; cf. A dag̠rā
def: s.m. A kind of handwriting, the large thick characters in which the royal titles prefixed to letters, diplomas, or other public deeds are generally written; (hence) the royal titles prefixed to letters, diplomas, &c.; the imperial signature.

ety: inf. n. of طغو, or طغي 'To exceed just measure,' &c.
def: s.m. Disobedience, rebellion, sedition, perverseness, insolence.

ety: t̤ug̠yān, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Excess, exorbitance; overflowing, inundation, flood, deluge.
adj. (dialectic) = terā (see toh).
pron. (emphat. of tū), Thou, thyself; thou alone; — (in Braj, dat. & acc. of tū), to thee.
s.m. Snow, frost, &c. = tuhin, q.v.
s.m. Mist, dew; hoar-frost, ice, snow; cold; moonlight, moonshine (cf. tushār)

ety: inf. n. of طہر; see t̤ahārat
def: s.m. Purification; cleanliness, purity.
v.n. = ṭhusaknā and ṭusaknā, q.v.
adj. = ṭuṅʼiyā, q.v.
s.m. Merchants; (in Urdū) a merchant (generally sing. in Urdū, unless it occur in Arabic phrases, as malikuʼt-tujjār).
s.m. (f. ?), One line of a poem; a rhyme; a cadence; — a moment (cf. ṭuk)
subs: tuk joṛnā, v.n. To rhyme; to make verses
v.n. To rhyme; to concert (with); to sing the same song (as), to agree (with), side (with).

ety: Prk. ठोक्कं; S. स्तोकं, cf. ṭūk
def: adj. & adv. A little; a little while
subs: ṭuk-ek, adj. & s.m. A little, very little; — a little bit; a little way.

def: s.m. A small piece, a bit, a little, a particle, an atom (see ṭukṛā)
subs: v.t. To break to pieces, &c. = ṭukṛā-ṭukṛā karnā, q.v.s.v. ṭukṛā
adj. & s.m. A little; — a small piece ( = thoṛā-sā)
s.m. An outspoken answer, the plain truth, a decisive answer, a flat refusal
v.t. To break or divide in two; to settle, decide.

def: s.m. Fear of God, piety ( = taqwā; taqīya).
s.m. A small piece, &c. = ṭūk, q.v.; — a single beat of a drum.
s.f. = tugā-kshīrī, q.v.s.v. tugā.
s.m. A piece, bit, morsel, scrap, &c.; a part, portion, division, fraction, section, paragraph, clause; a bit of bread; subsistence, livelihood
subs: s.m. One who lives on or who devours scraps or leavings; one who sponges; a dependant
s.m. One who begs for scraps or morsels of food, a beggar
(-ko), To give a scrap or morsel of bread or food (to); to allot in small shares, to dole out
(-ko), To break, tear, pull, or cut to pieces; to hack, chop up, make mince-meat (of); to cut to pieces; to analyze
(-ko), To break or divide into pieces; to divide, apportion, parcel out
shafaq-kā ṭukrā, s.m. A beautiful or lovely creature.

def: = H ٿکرا टुकरा ṭukrā, See ٿکڙ ṭukaṛ and ٿکڙا ṭukṛā.
v.t. See s.v. tū.
s.m. = tushār, q.v.
part. adj. = tushit, q.v.

ety: Pehl. tokhm; Zend taokhman, rt. tuć; S. tokman
def: s.m. Seed; sperm; an egg; a testicle; origin, principle
subs: s.m. The seed of sweet basil, or of the mountain balm; a seed of cooling quality
adj. 'Bad seed'; of bad stock, low-bred
tuḵẖm-rezī, s.f. Scattering seed, sowing; tuḵẖm-pāshī karnā, v.n. To sow seed
s.f. Purslain; the seed of Ocymum pilosum; a cooling drink
s.m. Seed of lettuce, Lactuca sativa
wety: From Classical Persian تخم (tuxm, “seed; semen; origin”).

ety: for A. تخمة, from تخم for وخم
def: s.m. Indigestion, dyspepsia.
s.m. See تكه tukka.
s.m. An arrow without a head, but with a button or knob at the end; — a little hill, an eminence.
s.f. = H تکلا तुकला tuklā, s.m. A small paper kite (syn.guḍḍī).

ety: corr. of tarkash; but cf. tukka
def: s.m. A quiver.
s.m. = H تکل तुक्कल tukkal, s.f. A small paper kite (syn.guḍḍī).
s.f. A child's paper kite.

ety: prob. connected with tukka, and S. twak
def: s.m. (Orig. 'a button'), a loop, button-loop, button-hole, eye-loop.
A kind of small basket (used for grain, &c.).
s.m. A piece, bit, morsel, scrap, &c.; a part, portion, division, fraction, section, paragraph, clause; a bit of bread; subsistence, livelihood
subs: s.m. One who lives on or who devours scraps or leavings; one who sponges; a dependant
s.m. One who begs for scraps or morsels of food, a beggar
(-ko), To give a scrap or morsel of bread or food (to); to allot in small shares, to dole out
(-ko), To break, tear, pull, or cut to pieces; to hack, chop up, make mince-meat (of); to cut to pieces; to analyze
(-ko), To break or divide into pieces; to divide, apportion, parcel out
shafaq-kā ṭukrā, s.m. A beautiful or lovely creature.

def: = H ٿکر टुकर ṭukar, See ٿکڙ ṭukaṛ and ٿکڙا ṭukṛā.
s.f. See ṭukṛī.
s.f. A small piece, &c.; a small pane of glass; a piece of cloth.

ety: S. स्तवक+र+इका; but cf. ṭukṛī above
def: s.f. A party, band, troop; a division or corps (of an army); a flock (of birds).
v.t. To cause to be challenged, to have (one) interrogated; to have (one) taken to task, &c. (see ṭoknā).

ety: Prk. तुल्ली, and तुल्लं; S. तुल्यः and तुल्यं
def: adj. & adv. Equal; like, similar; alike
subs: v.n. To turn out or prove to be equal; to be weighed against valuable things which are to be given in alms; to sit straight and evenly balanced (on a horse or in a boat; — tul, here, is probably the past conj. part. of tulnā)
(-ke), To stand facing or opposed (to), to confront, be drawn up in battle array.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit तिल (tila), from Sanskrit तिल (tila), borrowed from a Munda language.

def: s.m. = tul, and tulya, q.v.
s.m. Twill, twilled cloth.

ety: inf. n. of طول 'to be or become long,' &c.
def: s.m. Length; longitude; prolixity; diffuseness; — adj. Long, tall; extended, extensive, prolonged, lasting long
subs: v.n. To assume length, to be drawn out, to be or become prolonged; to extend far or a long way
(-ko), To lengthen, to elongate, to make prolix, to spin a long yarn or rigmarole
or t̤ūl-ě-kālam, s.m. Length of discourse, many words, prolixity, circumlocution, verbosity

def: s.f. 'Cotton-tube'; a thick roll of cotton which is drawn out in spinning.

s.f. A balance or scale, a pair of scales; the balance as an ordeal; the sign Libra of the Zodiac; a measure or weight of gold and silver, 100 palas, or about 145 oz. Troy; the practice of being weighed against gold or any valuable substance (which is afterwards given to priests); the science of Statics
subs: s.f. Ordeal by the balance (the weight of the accused being first taken, certain prayers and ceremonies are performed, after which he is weighed again; if lighter, he is innocent; if heavier, or as at first, guilty)
s.m. The gift to a Brāhman of as much gold, silver, &c. as equals the weight of the body
s.f. An ornament of the feet or toes; a hundred millions
s.f. The entrance of the sun into Libra

or tulā-bīj, s.m. The berry of the Abrus precatorius, from which the goldsmith's or jeweller's weight in India is taken (the berry weighs about 1 5⁄16 grains Troy, the factitious weight about 2 3⁄16 grains).

def: part. adj. Weighed; poised, evenly balanced, in equipoise, in equilibrium; — tulā huʼā, adj. Idem
subs: v.n. To sit evenly balanced (in a boat, &c.), to sit straight or erect (on a horse; cf. tul baithnā); to be in equilibrium
s.m. A system of forces in equilibrium.

def: (prop. pl. of t̤alīb; but com. used in India for t̤alaba, pl. of t̤ālib), s.m. pl. 'Seekers after knowledge,' students.
s.f. = tulwāʼī, q.v.
v.n. (dialec.) To drop (as fruit, &c.); to move with tottering or feeble steps, to totter.
adv. Lengthwise; in length.

ety: caus. of tulnā; tulā˚ = Prk. तल्लावे(इ) = S. तुल्य+आपि; cf. talānā
def: v.t. To cause to be weighed, to have or get (a thing) weighed, valued, &c. ( = taulānā, tulwānā).
adj. Lengthy; very tall; prolix; — (in Pers. & Hind.) s.f. Great length; prolixity.
s.f. The shrub holy basil, Ocymum sanctum (said to have been produced from the hair of the goddess Tulasī, and held in great veneration by Hindūs)
subs: s.m. Name of a superior kind of fragraṇt rice (so called from its smelling like tulsī); — tulsī-dāna, s.m. A gold ornament
s.m. The leaf of the tulsī plant; — tulsī-dal ćaṛhānā (-par), To place or sprinkle tulsī leaves on the head of an idol or of a Brāhman
s.m. A bead made of the wood of the tulsī.

ety: tulā, rt. of tulā-nā+Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. Balancing, producing equilibrium, statical.

ety: tulā˚, base of tulānā+āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. The part of a (Hindūstānī) carriage which rests on the axle-tree arm and supports the body of the carriage.

s.f. A fibrous stick used as a brush by weavers to clean the threads of the woof (or used as a brush generally, cf. tur); a painter's brush or pencil; a rod, &c., dipped into a crucible to ascertain whether the contents are in fusion.
s.f. = tulī; tulā, qq.v.
s.f. Cotton; the wick of a lamp; a weaver's fibrous stick or brush; a painter's brush or pencil, or a stick with a fibrous extremity used as one; &c. ( = tulī, q.v.).
adj. Of or relating to length; long; tall.
s.f. A small balance, a steelyard.
s.m. Fox-grape; night-shade, Solanum nigrum.
s.f. A small bird (said to resemble the wag-tail).
s.f. The silk-cotton tree or Sīmal.

ety: tul˚ = Prk. तुल्ल(इ) = S. तुल्य(ते), pass. of rt. तुल्;+nā = Prk. अणअ = S. इनीयं
def: v.n. To be weighed, be balanced; to be suspended; to be drawn up in array (as one army against another); to lower; — v.t. To weigh; to value, appraise, &c. (See tolnā).
v.n. To become soft (with moisture, as a mud wall, or a suppurating boil, &c.), to become pulpy, mellow, or ripe (as fruit); to spout or gush out; to drip.

ety: inf. n. of طلع 'to rise' (as the sun, &c.
def: s.m. Rising, appearing (of the sun, &c.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طلوع (tulū'), from Arabic طُلُوع (ṭulūʕ).

def: s.f. The act o weighing; price paid for having (a thing) weighed, weighment fee.

ety: fr. tulwā-nā; aʼiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S.(इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. A weigher, weighman.

ety: doub. cause of tolnā; — tulwā˚ = tul+āw redupl. = Prk. आवे = S. आपि
def: v.t. To have (a thing) weighed, &c. = tulānā, q.v.
s.m. Of equal strength
s.f. Equality; likeness, sameness, resemblance, analogousness.
pers. pron., 2nd pers. pl., You
subs: an inflective base, in Braj, of the dat., acc., and abl. cases of tum; — tum-ni-soṅ = tum-se; — tum-ni-kauṅ = tum-ko.
ex: کیا تم مجھ سے محبت کرتی ہو؟
kyā tum mujh-se mohabbat kartī ho?
تم جیسے لوگوں کی ہی ضرورت ہے یہاں۔
tum jaise logõ kī hī zarūrat hai yahā̃.
People like you are needed here.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुम्हि (tumhi), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe), from Sanskrit युष्मान् (yuṣmān, acc. pl.) adopting the initial sounds of त्वम् (tvám, nom. sg.).

def: s.f. (m.?) A trinket, ornament, jewels; trifles; any new and pretty thing, anything curious and striking; — a fine woman, a belle; — intestines, offal; — a frame for pigeons to perch on

ety: from tumānā; āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Price paid for carding cotton.

r: tuman, tǒman, lit, tuman bāṅdhnā, kā, tuman-dār
def: s.m. The commander of a tuman
subs: s.f. The command of a tuman.

def: v.t. To cause to be carded, to have or get (cotton) carded or cleaned.

ety: v.n. fr. طمر, of which Form II. = ʻto fold or roll up'; — prob. akin to Gr. τόμος
def: s.m. 'A paper, &c. folded or rolled up,' a roll, scroll; a volume, book; an account-book; (met.) a long story; a heap
subs: (-kā), To make a long story (of); to make much (of little); to go to great lengths
The produce or collection of a land recorded in Government books.

def: s.m. The gourd Lagenaria vulgaris; a hollowed gourd (in which mendicants carry water, &c.; see toṅbā, tomṛī, tūmṛī, &c.).
s.m. Wide drawers (almost like petticoats); drawers, short breeches.

s.f. A long white gourd, Lagenaria vulgaris; a small hollow gourd (in which medicants carry water, &c.; cf. tumbā); a kind of pipe or musical instrument (made of the gourd) played on by snake-charmers, jugglers, &c. ( = tumṛī; tāṅbī, toṅbī).
An optional base of the oblique cases of the pron. tum (but not commonly used, except in the dat.-acc. tumh-eṅ, the form tum being preferred).
The gen. masc. of the pron. tum (f. -ī), Of you; your, yours.
s.m. Thread made of cotton that has been separated and loosened by the hand and then carded (as opp. to the coarser kind of thread made of akṛā rūʼī, or cotton that has been beaten only).

ety: tūm˚ = Prk. तुम्म(इ) = S. तुम्बय(ति), rt. तुम्ब
def: v.t. To pull (cotton or wool) to pieces and separate it with the fingers preparatory to combing
subs: v.t. intens. of and = tūmnā; (fig.) to pull (one) to pieces; to abuse roundly.

def: v.t. To push gently, to jog, nudge

ety: S. तुम्बि+ṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A hollow gourd, &c. ( = tumbī; tūmbṛī, tombṛī).
s.f. = tūmṛī, q.v.
pron. See s.v. tum.
s.m. Magnificence, state, grandeur, pomp, show (cf. dhūm-dhaṛakkā).
adj. & s.m. Tumultuous, noisy; excited, perplexed, confused; — tumultuous sound, tumult, uproar, clang, clatter, din; tumultuous or confused combat, mêlée, confusion
subs: s.m. Noisy dispute or altercation, excited controversy.

def: pron. (prov.), Thou ( = tū)
subs: v.n. To dispute, &c. = tū-tū karnā and tū-karnā, q.v.s.v. tū.

def: s.m. The tree Cedrela toona and its wood ( = tunna, tun, q.v.).
s.m. A quiver.
s.m. Ventris crepitus
v.n. To abort, to miscarry, to slip
adj. Little, small; thin, slight, slender; weak, delicate; effeminate; — s.m. A thin cake or fritter ( = tunkī)
subs: adj. & s.m. lit. 'A weak vessel'; shallow; empty; simple; unable to keep a secret; unable to drink much, easily upset by drink; — a simpleton; a tale-bearer, tattler
adj. Slight or weak in substance; poor of resources
adj. Whimsical; fretful, peevish, captious, touchy, irritable

def: s.m. = tūmiyā, q.v.
v.t. = tūmnā, q.v.
s.m. = lit. 'Having a snout'; the alligator, the crocodile (syn. magar).
adj. = ṭunj, q.v.
s.m. A protuberant belly; the belly, abdomen; — s.f. The navel
subs: tund-parimr̤ij, s.m. One who is in the habit of stroking his belly; (met.) a lazy man, a sluggard

def: adj. Quick, swift, rapid, fast, fleet; brisk, active; hot, sharp, acrid; hot-spirited, impetuous, hasty; severe, stern, acrimonious; fierce, furious, violent
subs: adj. Hot-tempered, passionate, irascible, violent-tempered, furious; fretful, peevish
s.f. Violence of temper, aptitude to unreasonable and excessive anger, passionateness, irascibility; fretfulness, peevishness
v.n. To become angry, be very displeased.

def: s.m. A hand or branch that has been cut off; stump of a branch, or arm, &c.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Handless, born without hands, having the hands cut off; — a person who is without hands or arms (whether maimed or born so); one whose hands are useless, a lazy fellow, one who won't work; — the knob in the back part of a turban
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a knob in the back part (a turban); — a turban which has a knob in the hinder part.

def: s.m. (?), Thunder; — the nightingale.
adj. Gorbellied, &c. = tundil; — s.m. A gorbellied or corpulent person.
s.f. The navel; stump of an arm, the upper or lower arm exclusive of the hands (but app. only used in this sense in the foll. comp.)
subs: ṭunḍiyāṅ ćaṛhānā, tunḍiyāṅ kasnā (-kī), To tie the hands behind the back; to handcuff
v.n. To have the hands tied behind the back; to be handcuffed.

def: s.f. The navel; a small branch (broken at the end and leafless), stump of a branch; stump of an arm.
s.f. Swiftness; briskness, activity; sharpness, severity, acrimony; impetuosity, violence, fierceness, fury.
adj. = tundil, q.v.
adj. = tundil, q.v.
adj. Having a large or prominent belly or navel; gorbellied, corpulent.
v.t. To tie the hands behind the back ( = ṭunḍiyāṅ bāṅdhnā).
adj. & s.m. High, elevated, lofty, tall, erect, prominent; strong; hot, passionate; — elevation, height, altitude, culmination; top, highest point, vertex, peak; — the tree Rottleria tinctoria
subs: s.m. A restive elephant, an elephant in rut
s.f. Name of a river in the Mysore territory (formed by the junction of the Tung and Bhadra rivers).

def: s.m. A vessel with a long and narrow neck; a body of troops.
s.f. Pecking, &c. = ṭuṅgār, q.v.
s.m. A scut, a short tail (cf. ṭunḍā).
s.f. Plucking (fruit, &c.); pecking; nibbling; picking (food); piddling.
v.t. To pluck (fruit, &c.); to peck, to nibble; to pick, piddle, eat squeamishly or with distate; to gather up (the dress).
adj. High, lofty; — s.f. A kind of basil, Ocymum gratissimum.
v.t. = ṭūṅgārnā, q.v.
s.m. = H ٿنہايا टुन्हाया ṭunhāyā, s.m. = H ٿنہائي टुन्हाई ṭunhāʼī, s.f. See ṭonhā and ṭonhāʼī.
s.f. = H ٿنہا टुन्हा ṭunhā, s.m. = H ٿنہايا टुन्हाया ṭunhāyā, s.m. See ṭonhā and ṭonhāʼī.
s.m. = H ٿنہا टुन्हा ṭunhā, s.m. = H ٿنہائي टुन्हाई ṭunhāʼī, s.f. See ṭonhā and ṭonhāʼī.
s.m. A quiver.
adj. Very small, tiny (cf. ṭunj)
subs: s.m. A small species of parrot; a paroquet.

def: adj. Very little (quantity); very small (size), diminutive, tiny, stumpy
subs: v.t. To stake a very small amount, to gamble with very little; to win by degrees.

def: s.f. A thin crisp cake (made of flour, water, and usually a little sugar).
s.m. A stump, stalk, stem; a button, knob; a nipple; the clitoris.

part. Goaded, tormented, hurt, vexed, injured; cut, cut down, broken, &c.; — s.m. The Toon tree, Cedrela toona (the wood of which bears some resemblance to mahogany, and is much used for furniture, &c.); — s.f. The flower of the Toon tree (used for dyeing a yellow colour)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀡𑀽 (taṇū), from Sanskrit तनू (tanū́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tanúHs, from Proto-Indo-European *tenúh₂s, from *tenuh₂- (“body”).

def: s.m. = ṭunḍā.
s.f. See s.v. ṭan.
s.m. = ṭunnā, q.v.
s.f. Ringing sound, sound, ring, tinkling, tinkle, clink.
v.n. To sound, resound ring; to clink, tinkle.
s.f. A musical instrument (stringed), a rude kind of guitar or inandoline.
s.f. A tent-rope; the cord of a curtain, &c.
s.f. A small gun; a musket.
s.f. = tupak, q.v.
s.m. Musketeer; rifle-man; a gunner.
v.t. To cause to be buried, to have (a person or thing) buried, covered, or concealed.
v.t. To cause to be covered, to have (a thing, &c.) buried.
s.f. A weaver's brush; the fibrous stick used by weavers to clean and separate the threads of the woof; a shuttle; a loom.
s.m. = tūl, q.v.
s.m. A mountain; Mount Sinai.
s.m. Ground, earth, dust.
adj. Earthen, earthy; earthly.

r: turag, tura, ga, lit, turang, turag-brahma-ćāryak, lit.
def: (i.e. tura+ga), s.m. lit. 'Going quickly;' a horse ( = turang)
subs: s.m. lit. 'the sexual restraint of horses'; compulsory celibacy, leading a life of continence in consequence merely of being without female society.

def: adj. & s.m. Mounted or carried on a horse, riding, equestrian; — horseman, cavalier.
s.f. A mare; the plant Physalis flexuosa.
adv. Quickly, speedily, swiftly ( = turt, q.v.).
adj. Excelling, surpassing, excellent, superior, chief ( = uttam).
s.f. = turaʼī, q.v.
s.f. The cucurbitaceous plant, and the vegetable, Luffa acutangula.

ety: tuṛā, base of tuṛānā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका(तिका)
def: s.f. Price paid for plucking fruits, &c., price paid for changing money, discount, exchange.
pron. adj. f. (prov.) = tumhārī, q.v.
s.f. lit. 'Made of cotton'; a quilt; a mattress ( = toshak).
s.m. A trumpet ( = turaʼī, q.v.).
adj. & adv. = tūrṇ, q.v.

ety: Pehl. turān; Zend tūra, fr. taurv = S. तुर्व; cf. तुरुष्क
def: prop. n. The name of the country to the north-east of Persia, generally called Transoxiana, Tartary, Turcomania, Turkestan.
s.m. Boiled rice kept in cold water over night and used next morning, rice-gruel (syn. kānjī).

ety: caus. of toṛnā; — turā˚ = Prk. तुट्टावे˚ or तुट्टाव˚ = S. त्रुट्य(ति)+आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To cause to break; to break, rend; tear, worry (as a dog does); to beat down, lower (price); to change (money).
adj. & s.m. Moving quickly; a horse ( = turag).
s.m. A horse ( = turang).
s.f. = turaqī, q.v.
s.f. lit. 'Going quickly'; a dragon-fly.
s.f. = turānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Turān; — native of Turān, a Tartar.

ety: S. tarang 'a wrinkle'; the change of vowel, &c. being probably due to the Arabic
def: s.m. A citron; an orange; — an ornamental tissue, an embroidered work.
